title: Today's Tech Brief: The Pitchforks Are Here
summary: Billionaires scramble to contain AI's populist revolt
imageAlt: Image from Axios for "The pitchforks are here": Billionaires work to contain AI's populist revolt - Axios
imageCaption: "The pitchforks are here": Billionaires work to contain AI's populist revolt - Axios (Axios)

body.text: I'm looking at a piece from Axios that caught my eye: 'The pitchforks are here': Billionaires work to contain AI's populist revolt. It seems the billionaire class is getting a taste of their own medicine. With AI evolving at breakneck speed, the concern is that it might just upend the very power structures that have allowed them to accumulate wealth and influence.

The article suggests that AI's rapid advancement could lead to a significant platform shift. Think of it like the early days of the internet, where new platforms emerged and disrupted traditional power centers. Here, AI could democratize access to information, tools, and services, potentially diluting the control of those who have traditionally held power. It's a bit like the tables being turned; the pitchforks, metaphorically speaking, are being wielded by the very people who might be impacted by AI's disruptions.

In other news, it seems that Google's DeepMind is making strides in solving one of the biggest challenges in AI: making machines that can learn and adapt like humans. This has huge implications for areas like healthcare and education. Separately, I've been following the drama around social media platforms and their efforts to curb misinformation. It's a tough battle to fight, but it's heartening to see these companies acknowledging the problem and working to address it.

My quick read: today's tech stories are less about one giant thunderclap and more about a lively weather pattern. A little platform drama here, a little artificial intelligence irony there, and suddenly the industry has enough material for a very caffeinated morning show.

sources.title: "The pitchforks are here": Billionaires work to contain AI's populist revolt - Axios
